cl_03_spma_index

Yuanyuan Wen, Pragati Dahal

2026-07-09

Purpose

Compute the Small-Producer Market Access (SPMA) index for every county from distance_SPMA_merged.csv, then map it as a US county choropleth.

The SPMA index

For county \(i\), let

  • \(d^{\text{FM}}_i\) = distance to the nearest farmers market (miles),
  • \(d^{\text{hub}}_i\) = distance to the nearest food hub (miles),

and define the three sales pieces from the county’s total sales all_sales:

\[ \text{DTC}_i = \text{all\_sales}_i \times \text{d2c\_sales\_pct}_i,\qquad \text{LocalInt}_i = \text{all\_sales}_i \times (\text{local\_sales\_pct}_i - \text{d2c\_sales\_pct}_i),\qquad \text{Int}_i = \text{all\_sales}_i \times \text{intermediated\_sales\_pct}_i . \]

Then

\[ \text{SPMA}_i = \begin{cases} \dfrac{\text{DTC}_i}{\left(d^{\text{FM}}_i\right)^2} \;+\; \dfrac{\text{LocalInt}_i}{\left(d^{\text{hub}}_i\right)^2}, & \text{if county } i \text{ has a food hub,}\\[2.2ex] \dfrac{\text{DTC}_i}{\left(d^{\text{FM}}_i\right)^2} \;+\; \dfrac{\text{Int}_i}{\left(d^{\text{hub}}_i\right)^2}, & \text{if county } i \text{ has no food hub.} \end{cases} \]

A county “has a food hub” when number_foodhub > 0. Note that when a county has no food hub its nearest food hub is, by construction, outside the county, and its nearest farmers market distance is measured from the county centroid — so distance_nearest_hub_mile and distance_nearest_farmersmarket_mile already carry the “within/outside the county” meaning.

Load the library

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(ggplot2)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

Step 1 — read the merged county table

dat <- read_csv("../data/outcome/distance_SPMA_merged.csv") %>%
  mutate(county_fips = sprintf("%05d", as.integer(county_fips)))

Step 2 — total sales (all_sales)

all_sales (total county sales, in dollars) is a multiplicative factor on the whole index. Until it is added to SPMA_index_variables.csv, we fall back to all_sales = 1 so the code still runs; then the index reflects the sales shares only. Once the real column exists it is used automatically.

if (!"all_sales" %in% names(dat)) {
  message("`all_sales` not found -> using all_sales = 1 (share-based index placeholder).")
  dat$all_sales <- 1
}

Step 3 — compute the SPMA index

spma_index <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    has_hub = !is.na(number_foodhub) & number_foodhub > 0,

    dtc_farms    = all_farms * d2c_farms_pct / 100,
    interm_farms = all_farms * intermediated_farms_pct / 100,

    term1 = dtc_farms    / (distance_nearest_farmersmarket_mile^2),
    term2 = interm_farms / (distance_nearest_hub_mile^2),

    # a county contributes whatever channels it has data for
    SPMA = case_when(
      is.na(term1) & is.na(term2) ~ NA_real_,           # nothing observed → NA
      TRUE ~ coalesce(term1, 0) + coalesce(term2, 0)    # sum what exists
    )
  )

# quick look
spma_index %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            n_with_hub = sum(has_hub, na.rm = TRUE),
            spma_na = sum(is.na(SPMA)),
            spma_min = min(SPMA, na.rm = TRUE),
            spma_median = median(SPMA, na.rm = TRUE),
            spma_max = max(SPMA, na.rm = TRUE))
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##       n n_with_hub spma_na  spma_min spma_median spma_max
##   <int>      <int>   <int>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1  3144        327     132 0.0000206       0.261 4706635.

Step 4 — US county choropleth

# lower-48 + DC county polygons (drop AK/HI/territories)
counties_sf <- counties(cb = TRUE, year = 2023, progress_bar = FALSE) %>%
  filter(!STATEFP %in% c("02", "15", "60", "66", "69", "72", "78")) %>%
  transmute(county_fips = GEOID) %>%
  st_transform(5070)

states_sf <- states(cb = TRUE, year = 2023, progress_bar = FALSE) %>%
  filter(!STATEFP %in% c("02", "15", "60", "66", "69", "72", "78")) %>%
  st_transform(5070)

map_df <- counties_sf %>% left_join(spma_index, by = "county_fips")

p <- ggplot(map_df) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = SPMA), color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = states_sf, fill = NA, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, trans = "log10",
                       labels = scales::label_number(), na.value = "grey88",
                       name = "SPMA\n(log scale)") +
  labs(title = "Small-Producer Market Access (SPMA) index by county",
       subtitle = "Higher = better weighted access to farmers markets & food hubs",
       caption = "SPMA = DTC/d_FM^2 + (local-intermediated or intermediated)/d_hub^2") +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey35"),
        plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey55"),
        legend.position = c(0.92, 0.25))
p

Step 5 — save

dir.create("../figures", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("../figures/spma_choropleth.png", p, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 150)

spma_index %>%
  select(county_fips, state, county_name = county_name_hub,
         distance_nearest_farmersmarket_mile, distance_nearest_hub_mile,
         number_foodhub, has_hub, all_sales,
         d2c_sales_pct, local_sales_pct, intermediated_sales_pct, SPMA) %>%
  write.csv("../data/outcome/spma_index.csv", row.names = FALSE)
